title: The Former Secretary of State Service Officers (Conditions of Service) Act, 1972
full_text: The Former Secretary of State Service Officers (Conditions of Service) Act, 1972 भूतपूर्व सेक्रेटरी आफ स्‍टेट सेवा अधिकारी (सेवा शर्तें)अधिनियम, 1972 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 1972-59 Act Number: 59 Enactment Date: 1972-09-21 Act Year: 1972 Short Title: The Former Secretary of State Service Officers (Conditions of Service) Act, 1972 Hindi Title: भूतपूर्व सेक्रेटरी आफ स्‍टेट सेवा अधिकारी (सेवा शर्तें)अधिनियम, 1972 Long Title: An Act to provide for the variation or revocation of the conditions of service of former Secretary of State Service officers in respect of certain matters and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions Department: Department of Personnel and Training Enforcement Date: 01-10-1972 Notification: 1st October, 1972, vide notification No. G.S.R. 420(E), dated 28th September, 1972, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(i). Last Updated: 08-04-2019 Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles The Former Secretary of State Service Officers (Conditions of Service) Act, 1972 Show all section Section 1. Short title and commencement. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Conditions of service of I.C.S. members of the Indian Administrative Service. Section 4. Conditions of service of I.P. members of the Indian Police Service. Section 5. Pay of I.C.S. members of Indian Administrative Service and I.P. members of Indian Police Service. Section 6. Retirement of I.C.S. members of Indian Administrative Service and I.P. members of Indian Police Service. Section 7. Pension of I.C.S. members of Indian Administrative Service. Section 8. Pension payable to former Secretary of State Service officers in Indian currency only. Section 9. Power of Central Government to adapt certain rules, regulations and orders. Section 10. Power to construe rules, regulations and orders. Section 10A. Power of Central Government to make orders in certain cases to ensure parity. Section 11. Power to remove difficulties. Section 12. Act to have overriding effect. Section 13. Saving of orders in respect of disciplinary matters. Schedule 1.
